Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Jul-18-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Laura Herron Weber, Esq. 510-452-5000 of Siegel Lewitter Malkani for Plaintiff.
Alison Eckstrom, Esq. 949-223-7000 for Defendant Marriot International through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff argues procedural unconscionability. Defendant agrees with the tentative ruling. The Court shares its comments. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT MARRIOTT INTERNATIONAL, INC., STARWOOD HOTELS & RESORTS WORLDWIDE, LLC, W HOTEL MANAGEMENT, INC., ROGER HULDI, JULIO FERNANDEZ'S Motion To Compel Arbitration.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendants Marriott International, Inc., Starwood Hotels & Resorts Worldwide, LLC, W Hotel Management, Inc., Roger Huldi, and Julio Fernandez's motion to compel arbitration is granted. Plaintiff Cynthia Hill admits that she signed the arbitration agreement. (Hill Supp. Decl. 6.) By submitting the Declaration of Human Resources Director Wendy Hunter and the Agreement and Plan of Merger, of which the Court takes judicial notice, Defendants have established that Marriott, as the acquirer of Starwood, may enforce the arbitration agreement as a nonsignatory successor-in-interest. (See RJN Ex. 1 [merger agreement]; Hunter Decl. 6 ["In September 2016, Marriott acquired Starwood Hotels & Resorts Worldwide, Inc., which included the W San Francisco."]; Jenks v. DLA Piper Rudnick Gray Cary US LLP (2015) 243 Cal.App.4th 1, 9 [allowing nonsignatory successor-in-interest to enforce arbitration agreement]; Marenco v. DirecTV LLC (2015) 233 Cal.App.4th 1409, 1417 [nonsignatory defendant may enforce arbitration agreement between signatory plaintiff and a corporation that was acquired by the nonsignatory defendant].)(see word minutes for full details) (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

A proposed order is submitted by email. Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Jul-10-2019 Continued To Jul-18-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs MARRIOTT INTERNATIONAL, INC., STARWOOD HOTELS & RESORTS WORLDWIDE, LLC, W HOTEL MANAGEMENT, INC., ROGER HULDI, and JULIO FERNANDEZ's Motion To Compel Arbitration:

continued to July 18, 2019 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 302. The court exercises its discretion to accept the evidence filed with the reply papers, but, pursuant to Jay v. Mahaffey (2013) 218 Cal.App.4th 1522, 1537-1538, the court affords Plaintiff an opportunity to respond to the new evidence. Any response to the Defendants' reply evidence must be filed by 12:00 p.m. on July 15, 2019.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
